articles.title: Ebola Outbreak Faces Challenges As Traditional Healers Remain First Choice For Many
articles.description:
Efforts to contain Ebola outbreaks across parts of Africa have improved significantly in recent years. Better treatment facilities, stronger surveillance systems and expanded vaccination campaigns have sharpened the ability of health officials to respond quickly when new cases are reported. However, a significant obstacle still exists: a lot of people still turn to traditional healers for assistance before going to clinics or hospitals.

Medical professionals warn that this delay can slow the identification of Ebola cases, complicate contact tracing and make it far harder to prevent the virus from spreading through communities.

Enduring Trust In Traditional Healers

In many affected regions, traditional healers are not fringe figures - they are respected members of the community who provide herbal cures, spiritual assistance and wisdom steeped in local customs. For years, they have functioned as trusted sources of care, forging ties that formal healthcare institutions sometimes struggle to replicate.

ALSO READ |Doctors Reveal Surprising Diabetes Link Behind Frozen Shoulder

The preference for traditional healers is not simply a matter of distrust towards modern medicine. In rural areas, hospitals can be hours away, transport is often unaffordable, and health facilities regularly face staff and resource shortages. When illness first strikes, visiting a local healer is frequently the most practical option available. According to a report by Associated Press, traditional healers and religious leaders are often the first point of contact during health emergencies precisely because of the deep community trust they command.

“When people do not trust the health system, they often go first to traditional healers, faith leaders, or people they already know. The danger is that many only reach the hospital when they are already very sick,” AP quoted Onesphore Bangenza of the aid group Mercy Corps as saying.

Cultural Beliefs And Health-Seeking Behaviour

Cultural frameworks also shape how communities respond to disease. During past Ebola outbreaks, some families attributed unexplained illness to spiritual causes or ancestral forces rather than a virus. This is compounded by the fact that early Ebola symptoms - fever, fatigue, vomiting and diarrhoea - closely resemble those of more common illnesses like malaria or typhoid, making it difficult to identify without proper medical testing.

ALSO READ | Can Eating 50 Eggs At Once Be Deadly? Jaunpur Case Highlights Serious Risks

“Some people still describe Ebola as something mysterious, spiritual, or brought by outsiders, rather than a disease that needs medical care,” Bangenza further said.

The World Health Organization (WHO) notes that the disease can progress to severe complications, including internal and external bleeding.

Aid workers operating in outbreak zones have reportedly encountered communities where Ebola is sometimes understood through a spiritual or supernatural lens, leading some families to exhaust traditional remedies before seeking clinical care.

Why The Delay Matters

These delays carry serious consequences. According to the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Ebola spreads through direct contact with the bodily fluids of an infected person. Every day a patient goes undiagnosed, the people around them - family members, caregivers, neighbours - risk exposure. Faster identification is not just about treating the individual; it is the foundation of containing the outbreak entirely.

articles.title: Who was Daveigh Chase? 'Lilo & Stitch' voice actor and 'The Ring' villain dies at 35 - Check cause of death
articles.description: Actress Daveigh Chase, famous for voicing Lilo in Disney's Lilo & Stitch and playing Samara Morgan in The Ring, has passed away. She was 35. According to her boyfriend, Roy Hernandez, Chase died after developing complications from meningitis, which led to a bloodstream infection and sepsis. She had been hospitalized earlier this month due to malnutrition.

articles.title: Alia Bhatt & Sharvari are total Alphas in YRF’s popcorn action entertainer - Alpha!
articles.description: Yash Raj Films has dropped the trailer of Alpha, headlined by Alia Bhatt and also starring Sharvari, that sets up a relishing, fun origin story of two deadly girls in a popcorn action entertainer! The trailer also unveils more of the character of Bobby Deol, a ruthless mentor to Alia Bhatt. Alia plays an assassin raised and built to kill who goes toe to toe with her mentor turned foe. It also reveals Anil Kapoor, who is teased to be connected to the Alpha program! Packed with high-octane action, slick visuals with a promise of a super fun, thrilling ride, Alpha positions itself as a true-blue entertainer that celebrates the fearless Alpha attitude of two girls - Alia & Sharvari. Sharvari’s character arc is purposely kept under wraps in the trailer as she plays a very important role in the film, but the trailer signifies her teaming up with Alia in a bid to stop Bobby Deol at any cost. Elevating the stylised, cool visuals of Alpha is the pulsating background score! Global Top DJ HUGEL's worldwide chartbuster track Jamaican Bam Bam has been incorporated in the trailer to bring in a relentless burst of energy to the trailer, symbolic of the adrenaline rush that Alpha seeks to generate when people see the film in theatres. Directed by Shiv Rawail, the film is set to release worldwide, exclusively in cinemas, on July 3, 2026. Catch the Alpha Official Trailer Here:
articles.source: Latest Movie Updates, Film News, Movie Reviews,pre

articles.title: 'Transfer Trimurthulu' Is a Family Entertainer with Thrilling Elements: Hero and Producer Vadde Naveen
articles.description: Under the presentation of Vadde Jishnu and the banner of Vadde Creations, actor-producer Vadde Naveen has made the film Transfer Trimurthulu. Kamal Teja Narla is making his directorial debut with this movie. The film stars Vadde Naveen, Rashi Singh, Shilpa Tulaskar, Vivek Raghuvanshi, Raghu Babu, Shivannarayana, Vadlamani Srinivas, Jwala Koti, Devi Prasad, Surya, Baba Bhaskar, Pramodini, Sathvik Raju, Anjali Priya, Gayatri Chaganti, and others in key roles. The film is slated for release on June 19. Ahead of the release, Vadde Naveen interacted with the media on Wednesday and shared his thoughts about the film. What made you choose the story of Transfer Trimurthulu? The core idea and story of Transfer Trimurthulu are very different and unique. I liked the story immensely. It has thrilling elements while still being a film that the entire family can watch together. My character in the movie also feels very fresh and different. Isn't Transfer Trimurthulu outside your comfort zone? The way audiences watch films has changed today. It is no longer enough to make only family dramas or love stories. Audiences are appreciating films that combine various elements and offer fresh content. Transfer Trimurthulu brings a new concept while remaining a complete family entertainer. Why did you decide to become the producer for your comeback film? I had immense faith in this story. I wasn't sure whether another producer would share the same confidence. It's not that someone else would have changed the story, but I truly believed in it. That is why I decided to produce it myself. Will Transfer Trimurthulu have entertainment elements as well? There is a lot of entertainment in the character of Trimurthulu itself. I appear as a constable, and the character suits that role perfectly. The film is not a regular commercial entertainer as audiences might expect. I play a constable who fights against problems while working within the system. We worked on the script for nearly a year. How realistic is Transfer Trimurthulu? The film is largely realistic. It is not a full-fledged investigative thriller. We included fights and songs only where they were necessary to the story. Have you noticed changes in film production compared to the past? Production costs have increased significantly, and many aspects of filmmaking have changed. However, we planned everything thoroughly in advance, so we did not face any major problems. The budget exceeded our initial estimate slightly. The OTT rights have already been acquired by the Zee team. Tell us about director Kamal Teja Narla. Kamal worked extensively on this story. We spent nearly a year focusing solely on the script. His approach to filmmaking and storytelling felt very fresh and innovative. What can you say about Rashi Singh? She plays the wife of Trimurthulu in the film. Raashi has delivered an excellent performance. I believe her character will impress everyone. Are you seeing the love audiences are showing you now? For the last twelve years, wherever I appeared, Telugu audiences would affectionately tell me, "Please act in movies again, sir." Now I am witnessing the love they are showering on both me and this film. I will always remain grateful for their affection. Which recent films and characters have impressed you? I liked Rangasthalam. I especially enjoyed the characters played by Ram Charan and Jagapathi Babu. I also liked Allu Arjun's character in Pushpa. More recently, I enjoyed Chiranjeevi garu's role in Man Shankara Vara Prasad Garu. Why did you choose a new director instead of working with an established filmmaker? I could have directly approached a big director. But first, I need to understand what my current market value is. Also, a reputed director must be interested in working with me. Once this film releases, I will have a clearer idea of my market position. What kind of appreciation do you expect after the release of Transfer Trimurthulu? I strongly believe audiences will say that I have made a good film. So far, nearly a hundred members of my family have watched it. They were all pleasantly surprised. Many of them expected me to make a film focused solely on promoting myself. However, after seeing the story and content, everyone was genuinely shocked. Among today's heroes, whom do you admire? I like the work being done by Nani and Sharwanand. They consistently choose good characters and films. I enjoyed Court very much. The young actor in Little Hearts performed really well. I also greatly liked Chiranjeevi garu's role in Man Shankara Varaprasad Garu. What kind of projects do you plan to do next? At present, I am focusing on solo hero projects and would like to continue doing them. However, if I like a story, I am open to multistarrers as well. If the content is compelling, I would even do a project specifically for OTT platforms. What can audiences expect from Transfer Trimurthulu? The story begins at a measured pace. However, once the central conflict starts, the narrative becomes gripping and remains engaging until the climax. The climax, in particular, is something no one will be able to predict.
articles.source: Latest Movie Updates, Film News, Movie Reviews,pre

articles.title: Mumbai’s monsoon wait continues as unfavourable weather conditions stall advance
articles.description: Mumbai's monsoon arrival is delayed due to unfavorable atmospheric conditions, with rainfall expected to be isolated for now. The India Meteorological Department anticipates increased activity over the Konkan coast around June 24-25. Meanwhile, the city continues to grapple with hot and humid weather, with temperatures significantly above normal.
